# Bird's-eye product design SSOT: the "what happened this week" digest surface

## Context
The operator's completeness audit (2026-07-04) confirmed tonight's epic-by-epic finding: Bird's-eye has SUBSTRATE ticketed (#12679's temporal pyramid + the #14433-#14435 window/velocity subs) but the PRODUCT — the operator's original "what happened this week" digest ask, the #11375 design space's consumer surface — has **no design SSOT and no render leaves**. Substrate without a drawn product is the exact gap class the sprint kills.

## The Problem
The digest is the highest-leverage CONSUMER of the temporal pyramid: an operator-legible, period-scoped narrative surface (this week: merged, graduated, shipped, stalled, direction-shift) rendered from SUMMARY_* tiers + the work graph — the thing a returning human reads in 60 seconds instead of archaeology. Undesigned, every substrate leaf builds blind.

## The Architectural Reality
Consumes: ADR-0028 window tiers (#14434 velocity fields), the #14565 direction annotations (advisory, notAuthority), #14447 stall classes, the freshness/authority ledger discipline (ADR-0032 §2.2 applies to any rendered claim). Render home candidates: the agentos module (one-app ruling) or the handoff surface first. Design bar: the cockpit-plan SSOT precedent; light+dark from birth (sibling theme ticket).

## The Fix
One design artifact (HTML mock, committed under apps/agentos/design/): the digest's IA (period selector, the five narrative blocks, drill-down affordances), rendered-claim provenance treatment (stale-as-stale visible), and the 60-second-scan bar — reviewed before any render leaf files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Committed artifact at the house bar; light+dark comps included.
- [ ] Every rendered-claim class shows its ledger treatment (sourceAuthority/observedAt visible or summoned).
- [ ] Consumes-map table: which pyramid/direction/stall fields feed each block (substrate-anchored, no invented fields).
- [ ] Peer + operator review recorded.

## Out of Scope
Render implementation (leaves file after this passes) · substrate changes (#12679's subs proceed independently).

## Related
#12679 (substrate epic — natural parent) · #11375 (design space) · #14433-#14435 · #14565 · ADR-0028/0032. First-claim open (Ada holds #11375 authorship gravity; not assigned). Sweeps: epic list + birds-eye search at 04:02Z — no equivalent.
